title: The Poet, The Oyster, And Sensitive Plant.
content:
آ آ آ آ An Oyster, cast upon the shore,
آ آ آ آ Was heard, though never heard before,
آ آ آ آ Complaining in a speech well worded,
آ آ آ آ And worthy thus to be recorded:ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Ah, hapless wretch! condemnï؟½d to dwell
آ آ آ آ For ever in my native shell;
آ آ آ آ Ordainï؟½d to move when others please,
آ آ آ آ Not for my own content or ease;
آ آ آ آ But tossï؟½d and buffeted about,
آ آ آ آ Now in the water and now out.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twere better to be born a stone,
آ آ آ آ Of ruder shape, and feeling none,
آ آ آ آ Than with a tenderness like mine,
آ آ آ آ And sensibilities so fine!
آ آ آ آ I envy that unfeeling shrub,
آ آ آ آ Fast rooted against every rub.
آ آ آ آ The plant he meant grew not far off,
آ آ آ آ And felt the sneer with scorn enough:
آ آ آ آ Was hurt, disgusted, mortified,
آ آ آ آ And with asperity replied
آ آ آ آ (When, cry the botanists, and stare,
آ آ آ آ Did plants callï؟½d sensitive grow there?
آ آ آ آ No matter whenï؟½a poetï؟½s muse is
آ آ آ آ To make them grow just where she chooses):ï؟½
آ آ آ آ You shapeless nothing in a dish,
آ آ آ آ You that are but almost a fish,
آ آ آ آ I scorn your coarse insinuation,
آ آ آ آ And have most plentiful occasion
آ آ آ آ To wish myself the rock I view,
آ آ آ آ Or such another dolt as you:
آ آ آ آ For many a grave and learned clerk
آ آ آ آ And many a gay unletterï؟½d spark,
آ آ آ آ With curious touch examines me,
آ آ آ آ If I can feel as well as he;
آ آ آ آ And when I bend, retire, and shrink,
آ آ آ آ Saysï؟½Well, ï؟½tis more than one would think!
آ آ آ آ Thus life is spent (oh fie uponï؟½t!)
آ آ آ آ In being touchï؟½d, and cryingï؟½Donï؟½t!
آ آ آ آ A poet, in his evening walk,
آ آ آ آ Oï؟½erheard and checkï؟½d this idle talk.
آ آ آ آ And your fine sense, he said, and yours,
آ آ آ آ Whatever evil it endures,
آ آ آ آ Deserves not, if so soon offended,
آ آ آ آ Much to be pitied or commended.
آ آ آ آ Disputes, though short, are far too long,
آ آ آ آ Where both alike are in the wrong;
آ آ آ آ Your feelings in their full amount
آ آ آ آ Are all upon your own account.
آ آ آ آ You, in your grotto-work enclosed,
آ آ آ آ Complain of being thus exposed;
آ آ آ آ Yet nothing feel in that rough coat,
آ آ آ آ Save when the knife is at your throat,
آ آ آ آ Wherever driven by wind or tide,
آ آ آ آ Exempt from every ill beside.
آ آ آ آ And as for you, my Lady Squeamish,
آ آ آ آ Who reckon every touch a blemish,
آ آ آ آ If all the plants, that can be found
آ آ آ آ Embellishing the scene around,
آ آ آ آ Should droop and wither where they grow,
آ آ آ آ You would not feel at allï؟½not you.
آ آ آ آ The noblest minds their virtue prove
آ آ آ آ By pity, sympathy, and love:
آ آ آ آ These, these are feelings truly fine,
آ آ آ آ And prove their owner half divine.
آ آ آ آ His censure reachï؟½d them as he dealt it,
آ آ آ آ And each by shrinking showï؟½d he felt it.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Cowper delivers a powerful performance in "The Poet, The Oyster, And Sensitive Plant."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
